occurrences.sentence: . Card zero of the Major Arcana, The Fool sits at the threshold of the Fool's Journey, the place where openness has not yet been narrowed by experience.

In the Rider-Waite tradition, The Fool is numbered zero — the only card that sits outside the numbered sequence of the Major Arcana, and the figure who begins the entire Fool's Journey that runs from card 1 to 21.
. Every hero's journey that the subsequent cards describe starts with this single, unmapped step.
In the Rider-Waite tradition, The Magician is numbered 1 — the first step of the Fool's Journey, the moment the unmapped leap of The Fool becomes directed action.
. Card 21 of the Major Arcana, the last of the Fool's Journey, it is the archetype of wholeness.
In the Rider-Waite tradition, The World is numbered 21 — the final card of the Major Arcana, completing the Fool's Journey that began with card zero.
<summary>What does The World mean in tarot?</summary>The World (card 21 of the Major Arcana, the final card of the Fool's Journey) is the card of completion, integration, wholeness, and fulfillment.
